refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume per hour or mass per hour.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is already focused on a single obligation: supporting achievement of a productivity target defined by the customer, measured in specific units. It does not include multiple obligations or implementation details, and remains solution-neutral by specifying what is to be achieved (supporting productivity target) without prescribing how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through operational testing and data collection during representative operations that the NGHE solution enables achieving or exceeding the customer-defined productivity target (in m³/h or ton/h) over the agreed evaluation period.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume per hour.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in mass per hour.

candidate_dps: Provide a controllable throughput mechanism to regulate processing speed
Implement a feedback system to monitor and adjust productivity in real-time
Design modular processing units that can be scaled or combined to meet productivity targets
Incorporate a scheduling and resource allocation strategy to optimize workflow
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a feedback system to monitor and adjust productivity in real-time

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a feedback control approach that can independently adjust system parameters to meet productivity targets without tightly coupling to specific processing mechanisms.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of reliable productivity measurement methods during operations
Risk that feedback system complexity may introduce latency affecting responsiveness
Assumes operational policies allow dynamic adjustment of processing parameters
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the measurement and control interface to ensure modularity and ease of integration with other system components.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system responsible for implementing productivity monitoring and adjustment features, including feedback control algorithms and real-time data processing.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data inputs and sends processed productivity metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends commands or adjustments to operational units based on feedback system outputs.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the feedback system logically fits within the core solution; minimal diffusion as it primarily interacts via Telemetry and Tasking interfaces.
Partial containment possible but would require extensive diffusion to other modules for data and control, reducing cohesion.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control rather than productivity feedback, leading to high diffusion.
Poor fit; safety services focus on alerts and safety, not productivity feedback, causing high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the feedback system DP, as it centralizes productivity monitoring and adjustment logic and interfaces directly with relevant data and control channels.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if some feedback control requires embedded vehicle systems.
Dependency on Telemetry interface reliability for real-time data may affect system responsiveness.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing a feedback system to monitor and adjust productivity in real-time directly addresses the FR of supporting achievement of the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume or mass per hour. The DP logically fulfills the functional requirement by enabling real-time control to meet or exceed the productivity target.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if some feedback control requires embedded vehicle systems.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating it is an appropriate and focused implementation boundary for the feedback system DP. The interfaces (Telemetry and Tasking) are well defined and limited, minimizing diffusion risk. However, there is a noted risk of diffusion if feedback control logic must be embedded within the Vehicle module, which should be monitored.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring reported productivity during representative operations to ensure it meets or exceeds the productivity target over the evaluation period, as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the feedback system DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with explicit module containment scores and interface definitions supporting ownership and responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document any required interactions or embedded control logic within the Vehicle module to manage diffusion risk.
Ensure robustness and reliability of the Telemetry interface to support real-time data needs for the feedback system.
Consider defining explicit interface contracts and monitoring mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules to manage potential overlap and maintain cohesion.

decision.justification: The current FR to DP and DP to IM mappings have passed gates with clear justification and manageable diffusion risks. However, given the high safety criticality and noted potential overlap with the Vehicle module, further decomposition of the FR into next-level sub-FRs is warranted to explicitly address and isolate detailed functional aspects, such as feedback control logic embedded in vehicle systems and telemetry robustness. This will enable more precise design parameters and implementation modules to mitigate diffusion risks and ensure safety requirements are met.
next_level_sub_frs: Adjust operational parameters to maintain productivity target
Coordinate feedback control with Vehicle module systems

revise.why: Clarify and document required interactions or embedded control logic within the Vehicle module to manage diffusion risk and maintain high cohesion and low diffusion in implementation modules.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the primary implementation boundary for the feedback system DP.
Telemetry and Tasking interfaces are currently well defined but require robustness improvements.
Vehicle module may require embedded control logic for feedback control.
open_risks: Potential overlap and diffusion risk with Vehicle module feedback control logic.
Robustness and reliability of the Telemetry interface under real-time conditions.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
Verification data showing productivity meets or exceeds target during representative operations.
Documentation of embedded control logic requirements within Vehicle module.
